varieties.name: Victoria
varieties.origin: England, 1837 (developed by Joseph Myatt, named for Queen Victoria)
varieties.traits: Mid-sized plant (90-120cm tall); stalks green at top grading to red/pink at base; thick, smooth stems (2-4cm diameter); 60-120cm stalk length
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive; late-maturing (May-August harvest); reliable cropper

varieties.name: Ever Red
varieties.origin: Modern Australian variety, developed for warmer climates
varieties.traits: 100cm tall; deep crimson-red stalks; evergreen foliage; compact growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: Year-round production in warm climates; best harvests spring and summer; highly productive
varieties.availability: Available from The Seed Collection, Australian online nurseries. Seasonally available (winter planting preferred).

varieties.name: Silvan Giant
varieties.traits: Robust, large plant; thick red stalks; larger stalk production than other varieties; vigorous grower
varieties.yield_notes: High yields; larger individual stalks; prolific producer

varieties.name: Sydney Crimson
varieties.traits: Medium-large plant; super red stalks; winter-producing variety; adapted to Australian conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Best production in winter months; good yields; reliable cropper

varieties.name: Wandin Red
varieties.traits: Medium-sized plant; bright red stalks; consistent production; compact habit suitable for smaller gardens

varieties.name: KangaRhu™
varieties.origin: USA (Gurney's Seed), 2020 - developed from heat-tolerant Australian germplasm; Plant Patent PP31,875
varieties.traits: 24-30 inches (60-75cm) tall; deepest, darkest red stalks of any variety; greenish-red leaf stems; heat-resistant
varieties.yield_notes: Outstanding - continues producing into summer when other varieties bolt; extended harvest late spring to mid-summer; great yields

varieties.name: Glaskins Perpetual
varieties.traits: Long, bright red stems at base tapering to green at top; distinctive coloration; medium-large plant
varieties.yield_notes: Extended harvest - early spring to late fall; one of few late-season varieties; can take modest first-year harvest

varieties.name: Canadian Red (Chipman's Canada Red)
varieties.traits: 3-4 feet (90-120cm) tall and wide; ruby-red stalks; large succulent stems; cherry-red color
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; harvest April-June; consistent producer
varieties.availability: Occasionally available from Australian nurseries importing international varieties.

varieties.name: Crimson Red
varieties.traits: 3-4 feet (90-120cm) tall and wide; bright crimson stalks; plump, thick stems; distinctive deep red coloration

varieties.name: MacDonald
varieties.traits: Large, vigorously growing stalks; bright crimson color; tender skin; thick stems
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive; wilt-resistant; root rot resistant; harvest April-June
varieties.availability: Occasionally available from Australian nurseries, particularly those specializing in heritage vegetables.

varieties.name: Valentine
varieties.traits: Broad, deep red stalks inside and out; thick, long stems; attractive appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Produces few or no seed stalks (energy goes to stalk production); good yields; reliable
varieties.availability: Occasionally available from specialty nurseries in Australia.

varieties.name: Hardy Tarty (Colorado Red)
varieties.traits: Reddish stalks running red throughout; celery-sized stems; 60-90cm tall and wide
varieties.availability: Seed available internationally; occasionally from Australian seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Timperley Early
varieties.traits: Early variety (harvest February-April in UK); pink-red stems with green tops; 60cm tall; longer, thinner stalks

varieties.name: Prince Albert
varieties.origin: England, 1840 (developed by Joseph Myatt; named for Prince Albert)
varieties.traits: Reddish-green stalks turn rose-pink when cooked; heritage variety 180+ years old

varieties.name: Linnaeus
varieties.origin: England, 1842 (developed by Joseph Myatt; named for Carl Linnaeus)
varieties.traits: Deep green, tender stalks; historically most important forcing variety; large plant; thin skin doesn't require peeling

varieties.name: German Wine
varieties.traits: Green stems with pink speckles; distinctive ornamental appearance; bred specifically for wine making

varieties.name: Strawberry
varieties.traits: Long, thick stalks; pink to red coloration; attractive appearance
